title: TapPay Server APIs
overview: TapPay Server APIs adopt the REST archetype, so all requests are sent using HTTP POST. The APIs cover credit-card and e-payment processing: Pay by Prime (charge with a one-time prime token from the frontend), Pay by Card Token (charge with a stored card_key/card_token, e.g. for recurring transactions), Refund, and Record (query trade records). Payment method categories are Direct Pay (cardholder enters credit card on merchant frontend), E-Payment (e-wallet accounts such as LINE Pay, JKOPay, Easy Wallet), and Token Pay (tokenized cards from Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay). 3D Secure transactions are supported by setting three_domain_secure=true together with frontend_redirect_url and backend_notify_url. After an e-payment or Direct Pay 3D transaction, TapPay sends a Backend Notify to backend_notify_url and a Frontend Redirect to frontend_redirect_url; the Record API can verify the latest transaction result.

environments.name: Sandbox
environments.source: Pay by Prime API (code-block Format comment: Sandbox URL)

environments.name: Production
environments.source: Pay by Prime API (code-block Format comment: Production URL)

security_schemes.name: x-api-key
security_schemes.details: x-api-key: YourPartnerKey. Header sent on every request; value is the partner_key, the authentication key for each individual partner. Header also requires Content-Type: application/json.
security_schemes.source: Pay by Prime API (code-block Header comment); partner_key field 'Authentication key for each individual partner.'

endpoints.summary: Pay by Prime API. Pay using the one-time prime token obtained from the frontend. If remember=true, the response returns card_key and card_token for future Pay by Card Token calls.
endpoints.source: Pay by Prime API (code-block Format comment URL)

endpoints.summary: Pay by Card Token API. Pay using a permanent card_key and card_token, skipping createToken and Pay by Prime. Used for recurring/fixed-amount transactions.
endpoints.source: Pay by Card Token API (code-block Format comment URL)

endpoints.summary: Refund API. Refund a transaction, reversing capture and authorization; supports partial refund via the amount parameter.
Record API. Get trade records, filtered and paginated.

Backend Notify (webhook). After an e-payment or Direct Pay 3D transaction is completed, TapPay POSTs a JSON transaction-result notification to the merchant-supplied backend_notify_url (Content-Type: application/json). If not acknowledged with HTTP 200, it is resent at 1,2,4,8,16 minutes (up to 5 times).

Frontend Redirect (webhook/redirect). After an e-payment or Direct Pay 3D transaction is completed, TapPay redirects the user to the merchant-supplied frontend_redirect_url with transaction-result query-string parameters (rec_trade_id, order_number, status, bank_transaction_id). Content-Type: html/text.

schemas.name: Pay by Prime Request

fields.name: prime
fields.description: The one time token returned from getPrime. Expires after 90 seconds.

fields.name: partner_key
fields.description: Authentication key for each individual partner.

fields.name: merchant_id
fields.description: Involved merchant's identifier as defined on Portal.

fields.name: merchant_group_id
fields.description: Cannot be used with merchant_id at the same time.

fields.name: result_url
fields.description: Required for e-payment and Direct Pay 3D transactions.

fields.name: result_url.frontend_redirect_url
fields.description: Frontend redirect URL. Must start with https.

fields.name: remember
fields.description: If true, response returns card_secret (card_key and card_token).

schemas.constraints: merchant_group_id cannot be used with merchant_id. Set timeout to 30 seconds.
schemas.source: Pay by Prime API (request parameter table)

schemas.name: Pay by Prime Response

fields.name: auth_code
fields.description: Authorization code. Direct Pay and Token Pay only.

fields.name: card_secret.card_token
fields.description: Card token for future Pay by Card Token.

enums: funding: -1 = Unknown, 0 = Credit Card, 1 = Debit Card, 2 = Prepaid Card
type: -1 = Unknown, 1 = VISA, 2 = MasterCard, 3 = JCB, 4 = Union Pay, 5 = AMEX

schemas.name: Pay by Card Token Request

fields.name: partner_key
fields.description: Authentication key for each individual partner.

fields.name: merchant_id
fields.description: Merchant identifier as defined on Portal.

fields.name: cardholder
fields.description: Information of the cardholder. Same sub-fields as Pay by Prime.

fields.name: card_ccv
fields.description: Security code. Cannot be used together with ccv_prime. Direct Pay only.

fields.name: ccv_prime
fields.description: One time token of security code from get-ccv-prime. Cannot be used with card_ccv. Expires after 90 seconds.

fields.name: is_merchant_initiated_transaction
fields.description: Whether MIT (true) or CIT (false). Default false.

schemas.constraints: merchant_group_id cannot be used with merchant_id. card_ccv and ccv_prime are mutually exclusive. Set timeout to 30 seconds.
schemas.source: Pay by Card Token API (request parameter table)

schemas.name: Pay by Card Token Response

fields.name: auth_code
fields.description: Authorization code. Direct Pay and Token Pay only.

enums: funding: -1 = Unknown, 0 = Credit Card, 1 = Debit Card, 2 = Prepaid Card
type: -1 = Unknown, 1 = VISA, 2 = MasterCard, 3 = JCB, 4 = Union Pay, 5 = AMEX
schemas.source: Pay by Card Token API > Response

schemas.name: Refund Request

fields.name: partner_key
fields.description: Authentication key for each individual partner.

fields.name: rec_trade_id
fields.description: Identifier for the transaction being refunded.

fields.name: bank_refund_id
fields.description: Self-defined refund record identifier (half-shaped English number, must be unique).

fields.name: amount
fields.description: Only required for partial refund. Instalment and T2P transactions do not support partial refunds.

schemas.constraints: Cannot call Refund API during capping time of acquiring banks. Set timeout to 30 seconds.

fields.name: refund_amount
fields.description: Amount being refunded. Except TWD, times 100.

fields.name: is_captured
fields.description: Whether the transaction has been captured or not.

schemas.name: Record Request

fields.name: partner_key
fields.description: Authentication key for each individual partner.

fields.name: records_per_page
fields.description: Number of records on each page, up to 200. Default 50.

fields.name: filters
fields.description: Restrictions on the trade records. Default None.

fields.name: filters.time.start_time
fields.description: Start time. time frame should be 90 days or less.

fields.name: order_by
fields.description: Criteria for ordering. Default attribute = time, is_descending = true.

schemas.constraints: filters.time frame should be 90 days or less. records_per_page up to 200.

schemas.name: Record Response

fields.name: status
fields.description: Response code. 2 indicates end of list (no more records).

fields.name: records_per_page
fields.description: Number of records on each page, up to 200.

enums: status: 2 = end of list (no more records)
schemas.constraints: The full per-record 'trade_records' array structure is not included in the provided source.

schemas.name: Frontend Redirect Query String
schemas.constraints: Request Header Content-Type: html/text. Frontend redirect could be faked; implement Backend Notify, and if it fails use Record API for verification.

schemas.name: Backend Notify Body

fields.name: auth_code
fields.description: Authorization code. Direct Pay and Token Pay only.

fields.name: transaction_time_millis
fields.description: Time when the bank handles the transaction.

fields.name: pay_info
fields.description: E-payment related information object. Only returned for e-payments transactions.

enums: pay_info.method: 1. CREDIT_CARD, 2. BALANCE (Electronic Payment Account), 3. POINT (Point Redeem), 4. DISCOUNT, 5. ACCOUNT_LINK (Bank Account), 6. CREDIT_BNPL (Buy Now Pay Later)
pay_info.payment_provider: EPI = LINE Pay Electronic Payment, TSP = non-LINE Pay Electronic Payment provider
schemas.constraints: Request Header Content-Type: application/json. POST to the merchant backend_notify_url. If not acknowledged with HTTP 200, resent at 1, 2, 4, 8, 16 minutes (max 5 times).

errors.meaning: Success. status=0 indicates success (request-level response code in the `status` field).
errors.source: Pay by Prime API > Response (status field)

errors.meaning: In Record API response, status=2 indicates end of list (no more records under the given filter).
gateway time out (referenced as 'error 421: gateway time out'; mitigated by defining a unique bank_transaction_id).

operational.detail: Set your request timeout to 30 seconds for Pay by Prime, Pay by Card Token, and Refund APIs.
operational.source: Pay by Prime API / Pay by Card Token API / Refund API

operational.detail: Every request must include the x-api-key header set to the partner_key (Authentication key for each individual partner), plus Content-Type: application/json. partner_key is also passed in the request body.

To execute a 3D secure transaction, set three_domain_secure=true and provide frontend_redirect_url and backend_notify_url. Only applicable to Direct Pay. After completion, TapPay redirects to frontend_redirect_url and sends Backend Notify to backend_notify_url. Recommended to call Record API (using rec_trade_id) before showing the result page.
operational.source: Pay by Prime API / Pay by Card Token API (3D secure notes)

operational.detail: If a Backend Notify is not acknowledged with HTTP 200, TapPay resends it at 1, 2, 4, 8, 16 minutes (up to 5 times). If all fail, a notification email is sent to the contact and technical mailboxes.

If remember=true, the Pay by Prime response returns card_key and card_token. Save them and call Pay by Card Token to handle recurring transactions. Not supported for Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay, JKOPAY, Easy Wallet, Atome, Pi Wallet, Plus Pay.
Do not call Refund API during the acquiring banks' capping time. Instalment and T2P transactions do not support partial refunds.

Direct Pay = cardholder enters credit card on merchant frontend; E-Payment = e-wallet accounts (LINE Pay, JKOPay, Easy Wallet, etc.); Token Pay = tokenized cards via Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay.

missing: No API-level version string is stated anywhere in the source (version = null).
No HTTP status codes are documented; all result codes are carried in the body `status` field. Full enumeration of status/msg error codes is via external reference.html, not included.
The Record API response per-record trade-record structure is truncated/cut off after number_of_transactions.
Many fields reference external reference.html / back.html pages for format rules, supported acquirers, currency limits, and e-payment support lists; those details are not in this source.
